mission.title: helm: helm upgrade fails with spec.clusterIP: Invalid value: "": field is immutable
mission.description: helm upgrade fails with spec.clusterIP: Invalid value: "": field is immutable. This issue affects 97+ users.

steps.title: Identify helm troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your helm deployment:
```bash
helm version
helm env
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review helm configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant helm configuration:
```bash
helm env
helm repo list 2>/dev/null || echo "No repositories configured"
```
When issue helm upgrade, it shows errors like, ("my-service" change from "clusterIP: None" to "type: LoadBalancer" without field clusterIP)

```
Error: UPGRADE FAILED: Service "my-service" is invalid: spec.clusterIP: Invalid value: "": field is

steps.title: Apply the fix for helm upgrade fails with spec.clusterIP: Invalid value: "":…
steps.description:
So https://github.com/helm/helm/issues/6378#issuecomment-557746499 is correct. Please read that before continuing on with this issue. If `clusterIP: ""` is set, Kubernetes will assign an IP. On the next Helm upgrade, if `clusterIP:""` again, it will give the error above, because it appears _to
```yaml
Error: UPGRADE FAILED: Service "my-service" is invalid: spec.clusterIP: Invalid value: "": field is immutable
```

steps.title: Upgrade helm to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade helm:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade helm helm/helm --namespace helm
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
helm list --all-namespaces
```

steps.title: Confirm helm upgrade fails with spec.clusterIP: Invalid… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
helm version
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: So https://github.com/helm/helm/issues/6378#issuecomment-557746499 is correct. Please read that before continuing on with this issue. If `clusterIP: ""` is set, Kubernetes will assign an IP. On the next Helm upgrade, if `clusterIP:""` again, it will give the error above, because it appears _to Kubernetes_ that you are trying to reset the IP.
codeSnippets: Error: UPGRADE FAILED: Service "my-service" is invalid: spec.clusterIP: Invalid value: "": field is immutable

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with helm installed or the issue environment reproducible.
